p.em: Standard POSIX Process State Transition Diagram (Intel Manual Grade)

div.p: If execvp() fails, call _exit() NOT exit().

Reasoning: Standard exit() flushes stdio buffers and runs atexit() handlers. Since a child process after fork() shares the file descriptors and memory state with the parent (until COW), a failed child calling exit() might flush buffers that contain parent data, leading to duplicate output or premature closure of shared resources.

g.title: Duplicate of parent memory; fork() returns 0
Duplicate of parent memory; fork() returns 0
